assessment: The page gets the fundamentals right: a dense warehouse-style table, a clear title with subtitle announcing '500 orders, 2311 line items', and a prominent truncation warning. But it fails as a pick list in practice. The baseline promises date, batch, location, picker columns — none are visible. Continuation rows (the SKU-00000002 rows with no Order or Client cells) create ambiguity about which order a line belongs to. Status badges dominate the right edge but there's no bulk action to mark PENDING→PACKED from the list, forcing a row-by-row drilldown. Density is fine, but information architecture is wrong for a warehouse operator scanning a 2311-line queue.
notable_strengths: Explicit truncation banner ('Showing first 500 results — use filters to narrow results') keeps operators from trusting a partial list.
Subtitle surfaces both order count and line-item count, which is the correct unit of work for picking.
Status filter defaults to 'All open', which matches the operator's default intent.

findings.title: Continuation line-item rows lose their order context
findings.what_is_wrong: Rows for SKU-00000002 ('Licensed Pre-Roll - cutover') appear repeatedly with blank Order and Client cells because they're secondary lines of the order above. A picker scanning mid-table can't tell which order a line belongs to without counting upward.
findings.why_it_matters: In a warehouse, mis-attributing a line to the wrong order causes mis-picks and short-shipments. The blank cells also break CSV export readability for anyone sorting the sheet.
findings.proposed_fix: Either (a) repeat the order number and client on every line with muted typography for continuation rows, or (b) group lines under a sticky order header row that shows order #, client, date, and a line count (e.g., 'S-1772839406310 · Sunset Dispensary · 2 lines'). Keep the flat row model for CSV but repeat order/client values in the export.
findings.functionality_preserved: Preserves `orders.getPickList` response shape and the documented columns (order, client, line items); only the rendering groups/repeats values.

findings.title: Baseline columns date, batch, location, picker are missing from the table
findings.what_is_wrong: FUNCTIONAL_BASELINE specifies rows include date, batch + qty, location, and picker. The rendered table shows only Order, Client, SKU, Product, Qty, Unit, Status. A warehouse picker cannot route themselves without bin location, cannot pick FIFO without batch, and cannot see due date for prioritization.
findings.why_it_matters: Operators must leave this page and open each order to find bin/batch/date, defeating the purpose of a consolidated pick list.
findings.proposed_fix: Add Location (bin), Batch, and Due Date columns. Default sort by Location ascending so the physical pick path is already ordered. Make Picker a compact avatar column on the right, assignable inline. If horizontal space is tight, collapse Unit into Qty ('1 LB').
findings.functionality_preserved: Uses fields already returned by `orders.getPickList` (baseline lists batch + qty, location, picker); this is a rendering change only, no router changes.

findings.title: No bulk status action; PENDING→PACKED requires leaving the list
findings.what_is_wrong: Every non-PACKED row shows a passive PENDING badge with no affordance to mark packed. There's no row checkbox, no bulk selector, and Export CSV is the only primary action.
findings.why_it_matters: The pick list's job is to be worked down. Forcing a drilldown per line on 2311 line items turns a 10-minute warehouse pass into hours of navigation.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a leading checkbox column with a select-all in the header. When one or more rows are selected, swap the Export CSV header zone for a sticky action bar: 'Mark packed', 'Assign picker', 'Print pick ticket'. Keep Export CSV accessible in an overflow menu.
findings.functionality_preserved: No baseline behavior removed — CSV export stays, status mutation already exists per order; bulk action just batches existing mutations without introducing new routers beyond what status transitions already require.

redesign_sketch: Header: 'Shipping Pick List' H1 with subtitle '500 orders · 2,311 lines · 1,842 pending'. Right-aligned: primary 'Print pick sheet', secondary 'Export CSV'. Filter bar on one row: Status chip group (All open / Pending / Partial / Fulfilled), Date range, Warehouse, Picker, Client typeahead, Reset. Truncation banner kept as-is. Table grouped by order with a sticky muted header row: 'S-1772839406310 · Sunset Dispensary · Due Oct 14 · 2 lines · [Assign picker]'. Line rows beneath show: [checkbox] · Location (bin) · SKU · Product · Batch · Qty+Unit · Status. Default sort: Location asc, then Order. Selecting rows reveals a sticky bottom action bar: 'N lines selected — Mark packed · Assign picker · Print tickets · Clear'. Status column uses color only for exceptions (partial = amber, pending = neutral outline, packed = green solid) so packed rows visually recede as the operator works the list down.
